name: smth
description: Give Claude a full browser: screenshots, DOM inspection, WCAG contrast analysis, and page interaction via a persistent Puppeteer/Chromium session.
long_description:
smth exposes 16 MCP tools across three areas:

**See** — read and analyze any web page: screenshots (`browser_see_visual`), visible text (`browser_read_text`), filtered DOM (`browser_see_dom`), fonts, colors, and WCAG contrast pairs.

**Act** — drive the browser like a user: navigate, click, hover, type, switch devices.

**Compare** — capture named DOM snapshots and diff them, or use `fetch_dom_content` to automatically strip shared chrome (nav, footer) and return only the unique content of a page.

tools.name: browser_launch
tools.description: Launch a browser session and open a URL. Always call first.

tools.name: browser_goto
tools.description: Navigate to a new URL without relaunching.

tools.name: browser_see_visual
tools.description: Screenshot the current page (any device, full page or viewport).

tools.name: browser_read_text
tools.description: Page text as Markdown plus interactive element selectors.

tools.name: browser_see_dom
tools.description: Lens-filtered compact HTML or text search with context.

tools.name: browser_see_colors
tools.description: All computed colors with usage counts.

tools.name: browser_see_color_pairs
tools.description: Text/background color pairs with WCAG 2.2 contrast ratios and AA/AAA flags.

tools.name: browser_see_fonts
tools.description: All fonts grouped by family, size, and weight.

tools.name: browser_click
tools.description: Click an element by id or CSS selector.

tools.name: browser_hover
tools.description: Hover an element and return a before/after diff.

tools.name: browser_type
tools.description: Type text into a form field.

tools.name: browser_dom_compare
tools.description: Diff two snapshots or subtract a background snapshot.

tools.name: fetch_dom_content
tools.description: Open a URL, auto-detect shared chrome, return only unique page content.

chromium_path.title: Chromium / Chrome executable
chromium_path.description: Path to the Chromium or Google Chrome binary. On macOS this is typically /Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome.
